NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the LORD; And He inclined to me, And heard my cry.
NKJV.2: He also brought me up out of a horrible pit, Out of the miry clay, And set my feet upon a rock, And established my steps.
NKJV.3: He has put a new song in my mouth— Praise to our God; Many will see it and fear, And will trust in the LORD.
NKJV.4: Blessed is that man who makes the LORD his trust, And does not respect the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
NKJV.5: Many, O LORD my God, are Your wonderful works Which You have done; And Your thoughts toward us Cannot be recounted to You in order; If I would declare and speak of them, They are more than can be numbered.
NKJV.6: Sacrifice and offering You did not desire; My ears You have opened. Burnt offering and sin offering You did not require.
NKJV.7: Then I said, “Behold, I come; In the scroll of the book it is written of me.
NKJV.8: I delight to do Your will, O my God, And Your law is within my heart.”
NKJV.9: I have proclaimed the good news of righteousness In the great assembly; Indeed, I do not restrain my lips, O LORD, You Yourself know.
NKJV.10: I have not hidden Your righteousness within my heart; I have declared Your faithfulness and Your salvation; I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth From the great assembly.
NKJV.11: Do not withhold Your tender mercies from me, O LORD;  Let Your lovingkindness and Your truth continually preserve me.
NKJV.12: For innumerable evils have surrounded me;  My iniquities have overtaken me, so that I am not able to look up; They are more than the hairs of my head; Therefore my heart fails me.
NKJV.13: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me; O LORD, make haste to help me!
NKJV.14: Let them be ashamed and brought to mutual confusion Who seek to destroy my life; Let them be driven backward and brought to dishonor Who wish me evil.
NKJV.15: Let them be confounded because of their shame, Who say to me, “Aha, aha!”
NKJV.16: Let all those who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; Let such as love Your salvation say continually, “The LORD be magnified!”
NKJV.17: But I am poor and needy; Yet the LORD thinks upon me. You are my help and my deliverer; Do not delay, O my God.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. I waited<S>6960</S> patiently<S>6960</S> for the LORD<S>3068</S>; and he inclined<S>5186</S> unto me, and heard<S>8085</S> my cry<S>7775</S>. <sup>I waited: Heb. In waiting I waited</sup>
KJV.2: He brought me up<S>5927</S> also out of an horrible<S>7588</S> pit<S>953</S>, out of the miry<S>3121</S> clay<S>2916</S>, and set<S>6965</S> my feet<S>7272</S> upon a rock<S>5553</S>, and established<S>3559</S> my goings<S>838</S>. <sup>an: Heb. a pit of noise</sup>
KJV.3: And he hath put<S>5414</S> a new<S>2319</S> song<S>7892</S> in my mouth<S>6310</S>, even praise<S>8416</S> unto our God<S>430</S>: many<S>7227</S> shall see<S>7200</S> it, and fear<S>3372</S>, and shall trust<S>982</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.4: Blessed<S>835</S> is that man<S>1397</S> that maketh<S>7760</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> his trust<S>4009</S>, and respecteth<S>6437</S> not the proud<S>7295</S>, nor such as turn aside<S>7750</S> to lies<S>3577</S>.
KJV.5: Many<S>7227</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>, are thy wonderful<S>6381</S> works which thou hast done<S>6213</S>, and thy thoughts<S>4284</S> which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order<S>6186</S> unto thee: if I would declare<S>5046</S> and speak<S>1696</S> of them, they are more<S>6105</S> than can be numbered<S>5608</S>. <sup>they cannot: or, none can order them unto thee</sup>
KJV.6: Sacrifice<S>2077</S> and offering<S>4503</S> thou didst not desire<S>2654</S>; mine ears<S>241</S> hast thou opened<S>3738</S>: burnt offering<S>5930</S> and sin offering<S>2401</S> hast thou not required<S>7592</S>. <sup>opened: Heb. digged</sup>
KJV.7: Then said<S>559</S> I, Lo, I come<S>935</S>: in the volume<S>4039</S> of the book<S>5612</S> it is written<S>3789</S> of me,
KJV.8: I delight<S>2654</S> to do<S>6213</S> thy will<S>7522</S>, O my God<S>430</S>: yea, thy law<S>8451</S> is within<S>8432</S> my heart<S>4578</S>. <sup>within: Heb. in the midst of my bowels</sup>
KJV.9: I have preached<S>1319</S> righteousness<S>6664</S> in the great<S>7227</S> congregation<S>6951</S>: lo, I have not refrained<S>3607</S> my lips<S>8193</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, thou knowest<S>3045</S>.
KJV.10: I have not hid<S>3680</S> thy righteousness<S>6666</S> within<S>8432</S> my heart<S>3820</S>; I have declared<S>559</S> thy faithfulness<S>530</S> and thy salvation<S>8668</S>: I have not concealed<S>3582</S> thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S> and thy truth<S>571</S> from the great<S>7227</S> congregation<S>6951</S>.
KJV.11: Withhold<S>3607</S> not thou thy tender mercies<S>7356</S> from me, O LORD<S>3068</S>: let thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S> and thy truth<S>571</S> continually<S>8548</S> preserve<S>5341</S> me.
KJV.12: For innumerable<S>4557</S> evils<S>7451</S> have compassed<S>661</S> me about: mine iniquities<S>5771</S> have taken hold<S>5381</S> upon me, so that I am not able<S>3201</S> to look up<S>7200</S>; they are more<S>6105</S> than the hairs<S>8185</S> of mine head<S>7218</S>: therefore my heart<S>3820</S> faileth<S>5800</S> me. <sup>falleth: Heb. forsaketh</sup>
KJV.13: Be pleased<S>7521</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, to deliver<S>5337</S> me: O LORD<S>3068</S>, make haste<S>2363</S> to help<S>5833</S> me.
KJV.14: Let them be ashamed<S>954</S> and confounded<S>2659</S> together<S>3162</S> that seek<S>1245</S> after my soul<S>5315</S> to destroy<S>5595</S> it; let them be driven<S>5472</S> backward<S>268</S> and put to shame<S>3637</S> that wish<S>2655</S> me evil<S>7451</S>.
KJV.15: Let them be desolate<S>8074</S> for a reward<S>6118</S> of their shame<S>1322</S> that say<S>559</S> unto me, Aha<S>1889</S>, aha<S>1889</S>.
KJV.16: Let all those that seek<S>1245</S> thee rejoice<S>7797</S> and be glad<S>8055</S> in thee: let such as love<S>157</S> thy salvation<S>8668</S> say<S>559</S> continually<S>8548</S>, The LORD<S>3068</S> be magnified<S>1431</S>.
KJV.17: But I am poor<S>6041</S> and needy<S>34</S>; yet the Lord<S>136</S> thinketh<S>2803</S> upon me: thou art my help<S>5833</S> and my deliverer<S>6403</S>; make no tarrying<S>309</S>, O my God<S>430</S>.

ESV.1: I waited patiently for the LORD; he inclined to me and heard my cry.
ESV.2: He drew me up from the pit of destruction, out of the miry bog, and set my feet upon a rock, making my steps secure.
ESV.3: He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God. Many will see and fear, and put their trust in the LORD.
ESV.4: Blessed is the man who makes the LORD his trust, who does not turn to the proud, to those who go astray after a lie!
ESV.5: You have multiplied, O LORD my God, your wondrous deeds and your thoughts toward us; none can compare with you! I will proclaim and tell of them, yet they are more than can be told.
ESV.6: In sacrifice and offering you have not delighted, but you have given me an open ear.  Burnt offering and sin offering you have not required.
ESV.7: Then I said, “Behold, I have come; in the scroll of the book it is written of me:
ESV.8: I delight to do your will, O my God; your law is within my heart.”
ESV.9: I have told the glad news of deliverance  in the great congregation; behold, I have not restrained my lips, as you know, O LORD.
ESV.10: I have not hidden your deliverance within my heart; I have spoken of your faithfulness and your salvation; I have not concealed your steadfast love and your faithfulness from the great congregation.
ESV.11: As for you, O LORD, you will not restrain your mercy from me; your steadfast love and your faithfulness will ever preserve me!
ESV.12: For evils have encompassed me beyond number; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I cannot see; they are more than the hairs of my head; my heart fails me.
ESV.13: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me! O LORD, make haste to help me!
ESV.14: Let those be put to shame and disappointed altogether who seek to snatch away my life; let those be turned back and brought to dishonor who delight in my hurt!
ESV.15: Let those be appalled because of their shame who say to me, “Aha, Aha!”
ESV.16: But may all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; may those who love your salvation say continually, “Great is the LORD!”
ESV.17: As for me, I am poor and needy, but the Lord takes thought for me. You are my help and my deliverer; do not delay, O my God!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the LORD; And He inclined to me and heard my cry.
NASB.2: He brought me up out of the pit of destruction, out of the miry clay, And He set my feet upon a rock making my footsteps firm.
NASB.3: He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God; Many will see and fear And will trust in the LORD.
NASB.4: How blessed is the man who has made the LORD his trust, And has not turned to the proud, nor to those who lapse into falsehood.
NASB.5: Many, O LORD my God, are the wonders which You have done, And Your thoughts toward us; There is none to compare with You. If I would declare and speak of them, They would be too numerous to count.
NASB.6: Sacrifice and meal offering You have not desired; My ears You have opened; Burnt offering and sin offering You have not required.
NASB.7: Then I said, 'Behold, I come; In the scroll of the book it is written of me.
NASB.8: I delight to do Your will, O my God; Your Law is within my heart.'
NASB.9: I have proclaimed glad tidings of righteousness in the great congregation; Behold, I will not restrain my lips, O LORD, You know.
NASB.10: I have not hidden Your righteousness within my heart; I have spoken of Your faithfulness and Your salvation; I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the great congregation.
NASB.11: You, O LORD, will not withhold Your compassion from me; Your lovingkindness and Your truth will continually preserve me.
NASB.12: For evils beyond number have surrounded me; My iniquities have overtaken me, so that I am not able to see; They are more numerous than the hairs of my head, And my heart has failed me.
NASB.13: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me; Make haste, O LORD, to help me.
NASB.14: Let those be ashamed and humiliated together Who seek my life to destroy it; Let those be turned back and dishonored Who delight in my hurt.
NASB.15: Let those be appalled because of their shame Who say to me, 'Aha, aha!'
NASB.16: Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; Let those who love Your salvation say continually, 'The LORD be magnified!'
NASB.17: Since I am afflicted and needy, Let the Lord be mindful of me. You are my help and my deliverer; Do not delay, O my God.

NLT.1:
I waited patiently for the LORD to help me,
and he turned to me and heard my cry.
NLT.2:
He lifted me out of the pit of despair,
out of the mud and the mire.
He set my feet on solid ground
and steadied me as I walked along.
NLT.3:
He has given me a new song to sing,
a hymn of praise to our God.
Many will see what he has done and be amazed.
They will put their trust in the LORD.
NLT.4:
Oh, the joys of those who trust the LORD,
who have no confidence in the proud
or in those who worship idols.
NLT.5:
O LORD my God, you have performed many wonders for us.
Your plans for us are too numerous to list.
You have no equal.
If I tried to recite all your wonderful deeds,
I would never come to the end of them.
NLT.6:
You take no delight in sacrifices or offerings.
Now that you have made me listen, I finally understand —
you don’t require burnt offerings or sin offerings.
NLT.7:
Then I said, “Look, I have come.
As is written about me in the Scriptures:
NLT.8:
I take joy in doing your will, my God,
for your instructions are written on my heart.”
NLT.9:
I have told all your people about your justice.
I have not been afraid to speak out,
as you, O LORD, well know.
NLT.10:
I have not kept the good news of your justice hidden in my heart;
I have talked about your faithfulness and saving power.
I have told everyone in the great assembly
of your unfailing love and faithfulness.
NLT.11:
LORD, don’t hold back your tender mercies from me.
Let your unfailing love and faithfulness always protect me.
NLT.12:
For troubles surround me —
too many to count!
My sins pile up so high
I can’t see my way out.
They outnumber the hairs on my head.
I have lost all courage.
NLT.13:
Please, LORD, rescue me!
Come quickly, LORD, and help me.
NLT.14:
May those who try to destroy me
be humiliated and put to shame.
May those who take delight in my trouble
be turned back in disgrace.
NLT.15:
Let them be horrified by their shame,
for they said, “Aha! We’ve got him now!”
NLT.16:
But may all who search for you
be filled with joy and gladness in you.
May those who love your salvation
repeatedly shout, “The LORD is great!”
NLT.17:
As for me, since I am poor and needy,
let the Lord keep me in his thoughts.
You are my helper and my savior.
O my God, do not delay.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. I waited patiently for Yahweh. He turned to me, and heard my cry.
WEB.2: He brought me up also out of a horrible pit, out of the miry clay. He set my feet on a rock, and gave me a firm place to stand.
WEB.3: He has put a new song in my mouth, even praise to our God. Many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in Yahweh.
WEB.4: Blessed is the man who makes Yahweh his trust, and doesn’t respect the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
WEB.5: Many, Yahweh, my God, are the wonderful works which you have done, and your thoughts which are toward us. They can’t be declared back to you. If I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be counted.
WEB.6: Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire. You have opened my ears. You have not required burnt offering and sin offering.
WEB.7: Then I said, “Behold, I have come. It is written about me in the book in the scroll.
WEB.8: I delight to do your will, my God. Yes, your law is within my heart.”
WEB.9: I have proclaimed glad news of righteousness in the great assembly. Behold, I will not seal my lips, Yahweh, you know.
WEB.10: I have not hidden your righteousness within my heart. I have declared your faithfulness and your salvation. I have not concealed your loving kindness and your truth from the great assembly.
WEB.11: Don’t withhold your tender mercies from me, Yahweh. Let your loving kindness and your truth continually preserve me.
WEB.12: For innumerable evils have surrounded me. My iniquities have overtaken me, so that I am not able to look up. They are more than the hairs of my head. My heart has failed me.
WEB.13: Be pleased, Yahweh, to deliver me. Hurry to help me, Yahweh.
WEB.14: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who seek after my soul to destroy it. Let them be turned backward and brought to dishonor who delight in my hurt.
WEB.15: Let them be desolate by reason of their shame that tell me, “Aha! Aha!”
WEB.16: Let all those who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let such as love your salvation say continually, “Let Yahweh be exalted!”
WEB.17: But I am poor and needy. May the Lord think about me. You are my help and my deliverer. Don’t delay, my God.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] I waited patiently for the LORD,
and he turned to me and heard my cry for help. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
He brought me up from a desolate [1] pit,
out of the muddy clay, ⓖ
and set my feet on a rock,
making my steps secure. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
He put a new song in my mouth,
a hymn of praise to our God. ⓘ
Many will see and fear,
and they will trust in the LORD. ⓙ
CSB17.4:
How happy is anyone
who has put his trust in the LORD
and has not turned to the proud
or to those who run after lies! ⓚ
CSB17.5:
LORD my God, you have done many things —
your wondrous works and your plans for us;
none can compare with you.
If I were to report and speak of them,
they are more than can be told. ⓛ
CSB17.6:
You do not delight in sacrifice and offering;
you open my ears to listen. [2]
You do not ask for a whole burnt offering or a sin offering. ⓜ
CSB17.7:
Then I said, “See, I have come;
in the scroll it is written about me. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
I delight to do your will, my God, ⓐ
and your instruction is deep within me.” ⓑ
CSB17.9:
I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly;
see, I do not keep my mouth closed [3]  —
as you know, LORD. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
I did not hide your righteousness in my heart;
I spoke about your faithfulness and salvation;
I did not conceal your constant love and truth
from the great assembly. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
LORD, you do not [4] withhold your compassion from me.
Your constant love and truth will always guard me. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
For troubles without number have surrounded me;
my iniquities have overtaken me; I am unable to see.
They are more than the hairs of my head,
and my courage leaves me. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
LORD, be pleased to rescue me;
hurry to help me, LORD. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
Let those who intend to take my life
be disgraced and confounded.
Let those who wish me harm
be turned back and humiliated. ⓗ
CSB17.15:
Let those who say to me, “Aha, aha!”
be appalled because of their shame. ⓘ
CSB17.16:
Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you;
let those who love your salvation continually say,
“The LORD is great!” ⓙ
CSB17.17:
I am oppressed and needy;
may the Lord think of me.
You are my helper and my deliverer;
my God, do not delay. ⓚ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. I waited patiently and expectantly for the LORD;
And He inclined to me and heard my cry.
AMP.2:
He brought me up out of a horrible pit [of tumult and of destruction], out of the miry clay,
And He set my feet upon a rock, steadying my footsteps and establishing my path.
AMP.3:
He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God;
Many will see and fear [with great reverence]
And will trust confidently in the LORD.
AMP.4:
Blessed [fortunate, prosperous, and favored by God] is the man who makes the LORD his trust,
And does not regard the proud nor those who lapse into lies.
AMP.5:
Many, O LORD my God, are the wonderful works which You have done,
And Your thoughts toward us;
There is none to compare with You.
If I would declare and speak of your wonders,
They would be too many to count.
AMP.6:
Sacrifice and meal offering You do not desire, nor do You delight in them;
You have opened my ears and given me the capacity to hear [and obey Your word];
Burnt offerings and sin offerings You do not require.
AMP.7:
Then I said, "Behold, I come [to the throne];
In the scroll of the book it is written of me.
AMP.8:
"I delight to do Your will, O my God;
Your law is within my heart."
AMP.9:
I have proclaimed good news of righteousness [and the joy that comes from obedience to You] in the great assembly;
Behold, I will not restrain my lips [from proclaiming Your righteousness],
As You know, O LORD.
AMP.10:
I have not concealed Your righteousness within my heart;
I have proclaimed Your faithfulness and Your salvation.
I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the great assembly.
AMP.11:
Do not withhold Your compassion and tender mercy from me, O LORD;
Your lovingkindness and Your truth will continually preserve me.
AMP.12:
For innumerable evils have encompassed me;
My sins have overtaken me, so that I am not able to see.
They are more numerous than the hairs of my head,
And my heart has failed me.
AMP.13:
Be pleased, O LORD, to save me;
O LORD, make haste to help me.
AMP.14:
Let those be ashamed and humiliated together
Who seek my life to destroy it;
Let those be turned back [in defeat] and dishonored
Who delight in my hurt.
AMP.15:
Let those be appalled and desolate because of their shame
Who say to me, "Aha, aha [rejoicing in my misfortune]!"
AMP.16:
Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You;
Let those who love Your salvation say continually,
"The LORD be magnified!"
AMP.17:
Even though I am afflicted and needy,
Still the Lord takes thought and is mindful of me.
You are my help and my rescuer.
O my God, do not delay.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I waited and waited and waited for God.
At last he looked; finally he listened.
MSG.2:
He lifted me out of the ditch,
pulled me from deep mud.
He stood me up on a solid rock
to make sure I wouldn't slip.
MSG.3:
He taught me how to sing the latest God-song,
a praise-song to our God.
More and more people are seeing this:
they enter the mystery,
abandoning themselves to God.
MSG.4:
Blessed are you who give yourselves over to God,
turn your backs on the world's "sure thing,"
ignore what the world worships;
MSG.5:
The world's a huge stockpile
of God-wonders and God-thoughts.
Nothing and no one
comes close to you!
I start talking about you, telling what I know,
and quickly run out of words.
Neither numbers nor words
account for you.
MSG.6:
Doing something for you, bringing something to you—
that's not what you're after.
Being religious, acting pious—
that's not what you're asking for.
You've opened my ears
so I can listen.
MSG.7:
So I answered, "I'm coming.
I read in your letter what you wrote about me,
MSG.8:
And I'm coming to the party
you're throwing for me."
That's when God's Word entered my life,
became part of my very being.
MSG.9:
I've preached you to the whole congregation,
I've kept back nothing, God—you know that.
MSG.10:
I didn't keep the news of your ways
a secret, didn't keep it to myself.
I told it all, how dependable you are, how thorough.
I didn't hold back pieces of love and truth
For myself alone. I told it all,
let the congregation know the whole story.
MSG.11:
Now God, don't hold out on me,
don't hold back your passion.
Your love and truth
are all that keeps me together.
MSG.12:
When troubles ganged up on me,
a mob of sins past counting,
I was so swamped by guilt
I couldn't see my way clear.
More guilt in my heart than hair on my head,
so heavy the guilt that my heart gave out.
MSG.13:
Soften up, God, and intervene;
hurry and get me some help,
MSG.14:
So those who are trying to kidnap my soul
will be embarrassed and lose face,
So anyone who gets a kick out of making me miserable
will be heckled and disgraced,
MSG.15:
So those who pray for my ruin
will be booed and jeered without mercy.
MSG.16:
But all who are hunting for you—
oh, let them sing and be happy.
Let those who know what you're all about
tell the world you're great and not quitting.
MSG.17:
And me? I'm a mess. I'm nothing and have nothing:
make something of me.
You can do it; you've got what it takes—
but God, don't put it off.

NIV.1:
Psalm 40
For the director of music. Of David. A psalm.
I waited patiently for the Lord;
he turned to me and heard my cry.
NIV.2:
He lifted me out of the slimy pit,
out of the mud and mire;
he set my feet on a rock
and gave me a firm place to stand.
NIV.3:
He put a new song in my mouth,
a hymn of praise to our God.
Many will see and fear
and put their trust in the Lord.
NIV.4:
Blessed is the man
who makes the Lord his trust,
who does not look to the proud,
to those who turn aside to false gods.
NIV.5:
Many, O Lord my God,
are the wonders you have done.
The things you planned for us
no one can recount to you;
were I to speak and tell of them,
they would be too many to declare.
NIV.6:
Sacrifice and offering you did not desire,
but my ears you have pierced;
burnt offerings and sin offerings
you did not require.
NIV.7:
Then I said, “Here I am, I have come—
it is written about me in the scroll.
NIV.8:
I desire to do your will, O my God;
your law is within my heart.”
NIV.9:
I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly;
I do not seal my lips,
as you know, O Lord.
NIV.10:
I do not hide your righteousness in my heart;
I speak of your faithfulness and salvation.
I do not conceal your love and your truth
from the great assembly.
NIV.11:
Do not withhold your mercy from me, O Lord;
may your love and your truth always protect me.
NIV.12:
For troubles without number surround me;
my sins have overtaken me, and I cannot see.
They are more than the hairs of my head,
and my heart fails within me.
NIV.13:
Be pleased, O Lord, to save me;
O Lord, come quickly to help me.
NIV.14:
May all who seek to take my life
be put to shame and confusion;
may all who desire my ruin
be turned back in disgrace.
NIV.15:
May those who say to me, “Aha! Aha!”
be appalled at their own shame.
NIV.16:
But may all who seek you
rejoice and be glad in you;
may those who love your salvation always say,
“The Lord be exalted!”
NIV.17:
Yet I am poor and needy;
may the Lord think of me.
You are my help and my deliverer;
O my God, do not delay.

NRSVCE.1:
I waited patiently for the LORD;
he inclined to me and heard my cry.
NRSVCE.2:
He drew me up from the desolate pit,
out of the miry bog,
and set my feet upon a rock,
making my steps secure.
NRSVCE.3:
He put a new song in my mouth,
a song of praise to our God.
Many will see and fear,
and put their trust in the LORD.
NRSVCE.4:
Happy are those who make
the LORD their trust,
who do not turn to the proud,
to those who go astray after false gods.
NRSVCE.5:
You have multiplied, O LORD my God,
your wondrous deeds and your thoughts toward us;
none can compare with you.
Were I to proclaim and tell of them,
they would be more than can be counted.
NRSVCE.6:
Sacrifice and offering you do not desire,
but you have given me an open ear.
Burnt offering and sin offering
you have not required.
NRSVCE.7:
Then I said, “Here I am;
in the scroll of the book it is written of me.
NRSVCE.8:
I delight to do your will, O my God;
your law is within my heart.”
NRSVCE.9:
I have told the glad news of deliverance
in the great congregation;
see, I have not restrained my lips,
as you know, O LORD.
NRSVCE.10:
I have not hidden your saving help within my heart,
I have spoken of your faithfulness and your salvation;
I have not concealed your steadfast love and your faithfulness
from the great congregation.
NRSVCE.11:
Do not, O LORD, withhold
your mercy from me;
let your steadfast love and your faithfulness
keep me safe forever.
NRSVCE.12:
For evils have encompassed me
without number;
my iniquities have overtaken me,
until I cannot see;
they are more than the hairs of my head,
and my heart fails me.
NRSVCE.13:
Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me;
O LORD, make haste to help me.
NRSVCE.14:
Let all those be put to shame and confusion
who seek to snatch away my life;
let those be turned back and brought to dishonor
who desire my hurt.
NRSVCE.15:
Let those be appalled because of their shame
who say to me, “Aha, Aha!”
NRSVCE.16:
But may all who seek you
rejoice and be glad in you;
may those who love your salvation
say continually, “Great is the LORD!”
NRSVCE.17:
As for me, I am poor and needy,
but the Lord takes thought for me.
You are my help and my deliverer;
do not delay, O my God.

NET.1: I relied completely on the LORD, and he turned toward me and heard my cry for help.
NET.2: He lifted me out of the watery pit, out of the slimy mud. He placed my feet on a rock and gave me secure footing.
NET.3: He gave me reason to sing a new song, praising our God. May many see what God has done, so that they might swear allegiance to him and trust in the LORD!
NET.4: How blessed is the one who trusts in the LORD and does not seek help from the proud or from liars!
NET.5: O LORD, my God, you have accomplished many things; you have done amazing things and carried out your purposes for us. No one can thwart you! I want to declare them and talk about them, but they are too numerous to recount!
NET.6: Receiving sacrifices and offerings are not your primary concern. You make that quite clear to me! You do not ask for burnt sacrifices and sin offerings.
NET.7: Then I say, “Look! I come! What is written in the scroll pertains to me.
NET.8: I want to do what pleases you, my God. Your law dominates my thoughts.”
NET.9: I have told the great assembly about your justice. Look! I spare no words! O LORD, you know this is true.
NET.10: I have not failed to tell about your justice; I spoke about your reliability and deliverance; I have not neglected to tell the great assembly about your loyal love and faithfulness.
NET.11: O LORD, you do not withhold your compassion from me. May your loyal love and faithfulness continually protect me!
NET.12: For innumerable dangers surround me. My sins overtake me so I am unable to see; they outnumber the hairs of my head so my strength fails me.
NET.13: Please be willing, O LORD, to rescue me! O LORD, hurry and help me!
NET.14: May those who are trying to snatch away my life be totally embarrassed and ashamed! May those who want to harm me be turned back and ashamed!
NET.15: May those who say to me, “Aha! Aha!” be humiliated and disgraced!
NET.16: May all those who seek you be happy and rejoice in you! May those who love to experience your deliverance say continually, “May the LORD be praised!”
NET.17: I am oppressed and needy! May the Lord pay attention to me! You are my helper and my deliverer! O my God, do not delay!
